candidate.objective: Highly experienced construction and pipeline superintendent with a proven record of managing large-scale civil, poly welding, and dewatering projects across mining environments in Western Australia. Skilled in leading multi-trade workforces, project budgeting, site safety compliance, and client relationship management.

duties: Reporting directly to the Managing Director and overseeing all operational aspects of company projects, including BGM installation, civil works (trenching for power), and poly welding operations, ensuring each project is delivered efficiently, safely, and in compliance with all applicable regulations and client expectations.
Leading and managing a workforce of 50 employees, 5 supervisors, and various contractors across multiple active projects to drive productivity and foster a positive, safety-focused workplace culture.
Coordinating all project activities including production, maintenance, and logistics.
Managing project budgets to ensure cost effectiveness and financial accountability.
Conducting regular site inspections and engaging directly with the workforce.
Providing technical and practical support to site personnel.
Ensuring all activities are conducted in compliance with health, safety, environmental, and operational procedures.
Planning, prioritising, and coordinating daily work schedules to meet operational and site requirements.
Monitoring performance, conducting inspections, and reporting on project outputs, system reliability, and improvement opportunities.
Working collaboratively across project teams to ensure seamless service delivery.
Leading and supporting continuous improvement initiatives to enhance system efficiency and performance.
Actively seeking new business opportunities to expand the company's project portfolio.
Preparing and presenting detailed estimates of job scopes and costings to prospective clients.
Operating excavators and heavy rigid vehicles across multiple Fortescue Metals Group project sites.
Installing dewatering systems in accordance with project specifications.
Ensuring all operations were conducted safely while meeting contract deliverables in relation to quality standards and schedule requirements.
Actively participating in pre-start meetings and daily work briefings.
Participating in Team Based Risk Assessments (TBRA) to ensure all hazards and risks were identified, communicated, and controlled prior to commencing work.
Maintaining a duty of care for self and fellow workers at all times.
Working in alignment with quality, health, safety, and environmental compliance requirements.

skills.detail: Experienced in overseeing large-scale civil, BGM, and poly welding projects from planning through to completion, including budget management, scheduling, and multi-trade workforce coordination.
Competent operator of excavators, IT loaders, front-end loaders, skid steers, water trucks, and boom-type elevated work platforms.
Holds advanced rigging, basic rigging, and dogging certificates with practical experience across construction and mining sites.
Proficient in WHS legislation and site safety management, including TBRA facilitation, Section 26 compliance, and working at heights requirements.
Experienced in preparing project estimates, scoping job works, and presenting costings to potential clients to support business growth.

certifications.details: High Risk Work Licence covering rigging and dogging operations.
Appointed Person under Schedule 26 for emergency management and explosives handling.
Certified operator of integrated tool carrier (IT) loaders.
Certificate IV in Leadership and Management.

referees: Professional references available upon request. Please contact ACM Resources to facilitate introduction.
